message:
fix(mcp): validate CreateIssueTool input through IssueValidator

`CreateIssueTool` was building an `Issue` and handing it directly to
`storage.create_issue`, bypassing `IssueValidator::validate(&issue)`.
The CLI `q` (quick-capture) path was tightened in commit 2acaf5cc
to enforce the same field-level limits before persistence; this
commit lands the matching parity fix on the MCP side so an MCP
client cannot smuggle in an issue (e.g. > 100KB description) that
the CLI would have rejected.

Fix: between the `Issue` construction and `storage.create_issue` in
`CreateIssueTool::call`, call `IssueValidator::validate(&issue)` and
map the validation error chain through
`BeadsError::from_validation_errors` and then `beads_to_mcp` so the
MCP client sees the standard `InvalidParams` shape with a
human-readable message.

Test `create_issue_rejects_description_over_validator_limit_without_-
persisting`:

- Builds a `BeadsState` rooted in a `TempDir` via a new
  `mcp_test_state` helper that wires up the on-disk db + jsonl paths
  the same way the production state does, and uses `br` as the
  issue prefix.
- Invokes the tool with a 102_401-char description (one byte past
  the 100KB cap).
- Asserts the returned `McpError` has `code = McpErrorCode::Invalid-
  Params` and a message that includes both `description` and
  `exceeds 100KB` so the failure reason is unambiguous to MCP
  consumers.
- Reopens the storage handle independently and asserts
  `get_all_ids()` is empty — proves nothing leaked through to the
  database before validation rejected it.

The test module imports grow to bring in `Arc`, `TempDir`,
`McpContext`, `Cx`, `ToolHandler`, `McpErrorCode`, the new
`CreateIssueTool` re-export, and the `BeadsState` type so the
fixture compiles standalone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
